prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Forsooth, in the third quarter after the Great Replenishment of Printer Parchments, the Guild's sorcery box—hight “SaaS”—hath bartered intangibles at an increment of 27 percent, though no cart nor mule was laden with goods.  
The council of number-sages cried that the Realm’s debt-to-magic ratio remaineth spotless, for they hath shifted every golden bezant to a ledgered ether called “Deferred Revenue,” where coin dwelleth among clouds yet keeps the fragrance of mint.  
In the same moon cycle, the peasantry did beseech for “on-premise” comforts, but were chased away with the spell-word “Scalable,” which turned their complaints into harmless confetti that the wind forgot.  
Therefore the prophets predict a new crusade for Q4: to trap a million idle wyverns (or “trial users”) within gated pastures, unto whom shall be promised visions forever deferred, like after-dinner apples hung just beyond the donkey’s nose.
